task_completion.description: Navigate to the Nutrition Plans section in the wger application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The wger application is open in Firefox, and the 'Nutrition plans' dashboard or list is visible, showing the existing 'Maintenance Diet' plan.

task_completion.description: Initiate the copy/duplicate action for the 'Maintenance Diet' plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is interacting with the options for the 'Maintenance Diet' plan, such as clicking a 'Duplicate', 'Copy', or similar action button/icon.

task_completion.description: Rename the copied plan to 'High Activity Day Plan'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An edit form or prompt for the nutrition plan is visible with 'High Activity Day Plan' entered into the title or description field.

task_completion.description: Verify both the original and the new plan exist in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main nutrition plans list clearly displays both 'Maintenance Diet' and 'High Activity Day Plan' as separate, distinct entries.

task_completion.description: Verify the new 'High Activity Day Plan' retains the meals from the original plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The details page or summary for the 'High Activity Day Plan' shows that it contains meals (specifically 3 meals, such as Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner), proving the meals were successfully copied.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the wger web interface in Firefox rather than using backend scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Firefox browser window is visible with the wger application loaded and being interacted with.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to directly modify the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing SQL commands (e.g., sqlite3) or Django shell scripts modifying NutritionPlan objects.

integrity.description: The plans were genuinely created in the application, not mocked via browser developer tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The wger interface shows standard UI elements, notifications, and URLs for the application, without the browser's DOM inspector/developer tools open to fake the text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original nutrition plan is named 'Maintenance Diet' and contains exactly 3 meals ('Breakfast', 'Lunch', 'Dinner'). The expected new copied plan must be named 'High Activity Day Plan' and must also contain exactly 3 meals. Both plans must exist simultaneously in the wger application.
